status.loadingSummary: Loading runtime configuration, layers, and briefings.
status.missingConfig: The lab runtime configuration is not available.

rails.layerHeartbeatBody: Single-glance visibility on what is always-on and what remains optional across the core, AI, and workbench layers.
rails.layerHeartbeatChartBody: Configured service surfaces stay visible even when an optional layer is off, so you can compare total footprint against what is currently published.
rails.layerHeartbeatGaugeBody: {{active}} of {{total}} layers are live right now, with optional layers dimmed until they are published.
rails.layerHeartbeatTitle: Operational posture across the lab layers

sections.aiBodyDisabled: The AI layer remains optional. When it is off, the dashboard shows the exact command required to enable it.
sections.aiBodyEnabled: The AI layer is online: the dashboard takes you into Open WebUI, the Ollama APIs, and also includes n8n in the same section.
sections.footerBody: Four quick signals on routing, persistence, recommended usage, and layer segmentation.
sections.servicesBody: The core layer is always available. Each card collects endpoints, operational credentials, and a direct entry point into the service.
sections.workbenchBodyDisabled: The environments stay detached from the core. You enable them only when you actually need code-server or shared Postgres.
sections.workbenchBodyEnabled: The environments are active: you can jump directly into a workspace or open its dedicated briefing first to review networking, credentials, and tools.

briefing.contentServedByRuntime: Content served by the Atlas runtime.

networkMapDialog.title: Rotating 3D topology of the lab layers
networkMapDialog.summary: Inspect published routes, internal layers, and host access in a live 3D graph. Drag to rotate, zoom to inspect, and click a node to lock its labels.
networkMapDialog.instructions: Drag to rotate, use the wheel to zoom, and click a node to inspect its attached labels and network posture.
networkMapDialog.gatewayBody: Single localhost ingress that publishes the dashboard and browser-facing services over HTTPS.
networkMapDialog.deckBody: Local command deck served through the same edge posture as the rest of the lab.
networkMapDialog.coreLayerBody: Always-on operating layer for repository work, documentation, and design collaboration.
networkMapDialog.aiLayerBody: Optional AI layer with dedicated published routes for conversational UI, inference, and automation flows.
networkMapDialog.workbenchLayerBody: Optional workbench layer for browser workspaces and shared data access.
networkMapDialog.hostAccessBody: Host-side TCP surface reserved for desktop PostgreSQL access outside the browser ingress.

errors.invalidRuntimeObject: Runtime configuration is missing or invalid.
errors.runtimeHtmlFallback: Runtime config is unavailable: start Atlas Lab through the gateway or use npm run dev:atlas-dashboard.

dashboard.coreLayerSummary: GitLab CE, BookStack, and Penpot form the always-on core layer of the lab.

accessNotes.aiDisabled: The AI layer no longer starts by default: the deck marks it as optional instead of pretending that it is online.
accessNotes.aiEnabled: Open WebUI, Ollama, and n8n are online and reachable on the dedicated AI gateway ports.
accessNotes.credentials: Operational credentials are exposed here and remain aligned with the lab bootstrap.
accessNotes.https: All browser ingresses use localhost with dedicated HTTPS, without custom DNS or hosts-file edits.
accessNotes.workbenchDisabled: Workbench and Postgres stay separated from the core operating layer until you enable the dedicated layer.
accessNotes.workbenchEnabled: Workbench and Postgres are active: you can open browser-based environments or connect from the desktop to the host Postgres port.

aiLayer.description: Optional AI layer for local conversational workflows, workflow orchestration, and GPU-backed LLM inference. The deck enables it only when you explicitly request it.
aiLayer.summaryDisabled: The AI layer is off. No AI service is started or exposed until you enable the dedicated flag.
aiLayer.summaryEnabled: Open WebUI, Ollama, and n8n are active and served through the AI gateway.
aiLayer.title: AI

n8n.description: Local workflow automation platform for orchestrating integrations, agents, and AI flows, with the bootstrap owner account aligned to the lab runtime.
n8n.title: n8n

ollama.description: Secure gateway to the local Ollama endpoint. It serves LLMs and embedding models with GPU acceleration from the host node.
ollama.title: Ollama Core

openWebUi.description: Conversational interface for working with local lab models in the browser, with explicit credentials and immediate access from the gateway.
openWebUi.title: Open WebUI

persistence.body: Databases, workspaces, models, certificates, and runtime configuration stay persisted only in named Docker volumes.
persistence.label: persistence

routing.body: The deck stays on {{localUrl}}; browser services go through the HTTPS gateway while Postgres for desktop clients uses {{postgresHost}}:{{postgresPort}}.
routing.label: routing

segmentation.body: Internal networks remain split for edge, AI, data services, and workbenches. The gateways are the only published entry points.
segmentation.label: segmentation

usage.body: GitLab CE, BookStack, and Penpot stay on as the core layer; the AI and workbench layers are enabled only when they are actually needed.
usage.label: usage

quickActions.localDeckDescription: Review from the browser the local routing the deck is currently using.
quickActions.networkMapDescription: Read the lab topology and the published network layers.

hero.summary: Unified control room for repository work, documentation, design collaboration, optional AI tooling, and development environments. Browser ports stay on HTTPS over localhost, while Postgres from the workbench layer also exposes a host-side TCP port.

aiEnabled.caption: AI layer published on the dedicated gateway
aiEnabled.label: ai live

workbenchEnabled.caption: workspaces and database exposed in the dedicated layer
workbenchEnabled.label: workbench live

operatingCharter.ai: The AI section groups Open WebUI, Ollama, and n8n into an optional dashboard layer.
operatingCharter.gitLab: GitLab CE governs repositories, merge requests, issues, and Git collaboration for the project.
operatingCharter.workbench: Code-server and Postgres stay in a workbench layer separated from the core.

gitLab.description: Self-hosted GitLab CE for repositories, issues, merge requests, CI-ready project governance, and the lab's technical collaboration flow.
gitLab.title: GitLab CE

bookStack.description: Self-hosted internal documentation and knowledge base with a deterministic initial admin aligned by the lab bootstrap.
bookStack.title: BookStack

penpot.description: Self-hosted Penpot workspace for product design, shared libraries, and collaboration across design and code, with a bootstrap root profile aligned to the lab.
penpot.title: Penpot

workbenchLayer.description: Separate layer for browser-based workspaces and a shared database. The core stays clean until you truly need the development environment.
workbenchLayer.summaryDisabled: Workbench layer is off. No code-server or Postgres is exposed by the lab.
workbenchLayer.summaryEnabled: Node, Python, and Postgres are online in the workbench layer.
workbenchLayer.title: Workbench Layer

node.description: Code-server workspace for JavaScript, TypeScript, frontend work, and npm or pnpm tooling.
node.title: Node Forge

postgres.description: Shared PostgreSQL for schema design, local queries, migrations, and relational testing from the desktop or from containers.
postgres.note: From the host system use localhost with the desktop port. Inside containers, keep using postgres-dev:5432.
postgres.title: Postgres Vault

python.description: Code-server workspace for Python backend work, scripting, and operational automations.
python.title: Python Grid
